facts.text: Die Hard (1988) was based on the novel 'Nothing Lasts Forever' by Roderick Thorp, which was itself a sequel to 'The Detective,' previously adapted into a 1968 Frank Sinatra film.
Tom Cruise performed a HALO jump from 25,000 feet for Mission: Impossible – Fallout, requiring over 100 skydives to get the shot right.
Bruce Lee could perform one-inch punches that sent opponents flying backward several feet, a technique demonstrated at the 1964 Long Beach International Karate Championships.
The Marvel Cinematic Universe has grossed over $29 billion worldwide, making it the highest-grossing film franchise in history.
Arnold Schwarzenegger's iconic line 'I'll be back' from The Terminator was originally scripted as 'I'll come back,' but Arnold felt the revised version sounded more machine-like.

Harrison Ford improvised the famous scene in Raiders of the Lost Ark where Indiana Jones shoots the swordsman, because Ford was suffering from dysentery and couldn't perform the elaborate fight scene.
Jackie Chan has broken his nose three times, most of his fingers, both cheekbones, and his skull while performing his own stunts throughout his career.
The first James Bond film, Dr. No, was released on October 5, 1962, with Sean Connery in the lead role.
Keanu Reeves trained for four months in judo, jiu-jitsu, and tactical weapons handling to prepare for the first John Wick film.
Mad Max: Fury Road used over 150 real vehicles in its production, with minimal CGI for the car chase sequences.
The Fast & Furious franchise started with a $38 million budget for the original film and has since grown into a multi-billion dollar series spanning over ten films.
Sylvester Stallone wrote the screenplay for Rocky in just three and a half days and refused to sell it unless he could star in the film.
The original Top Gun (1986) led to a 500% increase in U.S. Navy recruitment inquiries the year it was released.
Christopher Nolan's The Dark Knight was the first major feature film to use IMAX cameras for select sequences, with nearly 30 minutes shot in the large format.
Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson became the highest-paid actor in Hollywood, earning an estimated $87.5 million in 2020 according to Forbes.
The Raid: Redemption (2011) showcased the Indonesian martial art Pencak Silat to international audiences and is considered one of the greatest martial arts films ever made.
Sean Connery wore a toupee in every James Bond film he appeared in since he began losing his hair in his twenties.
Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl was originally based on the Disneyland theme park ride of the same name.
The 'Yippee-ki-yay' catchphrase from Die Hard was ad-libbed by Bruce Willis during filming.
Tom Cruise hung off the side of an Airbus A400M as it took off for a stunt in Mission: Impossible – Rogue Nation, with only a harness keeping him attached.
The John Wick franchise popularized the term 'gun-fu,' a hybrid fighting style combining firearms with martial arts choreography.
Predator (1987) originally featured Jean-Claude Van Damme in the Predator suit, but he was replaced after complaints about the costume design and his height.
Robert Downey Jr.'s portrayal of Tony Stark in Iron Man (2008) launched the Marvel Cinematic Universe and revitalized his career.
Jason Statham was a member of Britain's National Diving Squad for twelve years before becoming an actor, competing at the 1990 Commonwealth Games.
The TV series 24 popularized the real-time storytelling format, with each season covering a single 24-hour day across 24 episodes.
Bruce Willis was paid $5 million for Die Hard, which was considered a huge gamble since he was primarily known as a TV comedy actor at the time.
Kill Bill: Volume 1 features the 'Crazy 88' fight scene, which took eight weeks to choreograph and film.
The National Treasure franchise grossed over $800 million worldwide across its two films, turning Nicolas Cage into an adventure movie icon.
Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (2000) became the highest-grossing foreign-language film in American history at the time of its release.
Lethal Weapon's buddy-cop dynamic between Mel Gibson and Danny Glover set the template for countless action comedies that followed.
The Kingsman: The Secret Service church fight scene took 42 days to shoot and was edited to look like a single continuous take.
Batman Begins (2005) rebooted the Batman franchise after the critical and commercial failure of Batman & Robin (1997).
The stunt team on Mad Max: Fury Road included several former Cirque du Soleil performers who were used for the pole-swinging sequences.
Con Air (1997) used a real Fairchild C-123 Provider aircraft for its climactic crash-landing sequence on the Las Vegas Strip.
The James Bond franchise has featured seven different actors as 007: Connery, Lazenby, Moore, Dalton, Brosnan, Craig, and David Niven in a 1967 spoof.
Spider-Man: No Way Home brought together three generations of Spider-Man actors — Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland.
Jackie Chan founded the Jackie Chan Stunt Team, whose members train for years before being allowed to perform on film.
The Rock (1996) was Michael Bay's second feature film and is considered one of Nicolas Cage's best action performances.
Tom Cruise learned to fly a helicopter specifically for Mission: Impossible – Fallout, logging thousands of flight hours during production.
Ip Man (2008) reignited global interest in Wing Chun kung fu, with martial arts schools reporting surges in enrollment after its release.
The Bourne Identity (2002) was praised for its realistic, gritty fight choreography, which contrasted sharply with the stylized action films of the era.
Arnold Schwarzenegger earned $75,000 for The Terminator but $15 million for Terminator 2: Judgment Day, a 200x increase.
Peaky Blinders became a cultural phenomenon in the UK, influencing fashion trends and even increasing sales of flat caps.
Face/Off (1997) required Nicolas Cage and John Travolta to study each other's mannerisms to convincingly portray the body-swap premise.
The MCU's Infinity Saga spans 23 films released over 11 years, from Iron Man (2008) to Avengers: Endgame (2019).
Indiana Jones's fedora hat was designed by hat maker Herbert Johnson, and Harrison Ford wore the same style throughout the franchise.
The Boys (Amazon Prime) is based on the comic book series by Garth Ennis and Darick Robertson, offering a dark satirical take on superhero culture.
Rambo: First Blood (1982) was originally much more violent, but Stallone pushed for reshoots to make the character more sympathetic.
Daniel Craig's first Bond film, Casino Royale (2006), rebooted the franchise with a grittier, more realistic tone.
The Equalizer (2014) reunited Denzel Washington with director Antoine Fuqua after their collaboration on Training Day.
Taken (2008) revitalized Liam Neeson's career, transforming the dramatic actor into an action star in his mid-fifties.
X-Men (2000) is widely credited with launching the modern superhero film era, proving comic book movies could be both critically and commercially successful.
Bruce Lee's Enter the Dragon was released just six days after his death in 1973 and became one of the highest-grossing films of the year.
The Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle reboot (2017) earned over $960 million worldwide, far surpassing the original 1995 film.
Nobody (2021) starred Bob Odenkirk, who trained for two years in fighting techniques before filming, suffering multiple injuries during production.
Avengers: Endgame briefly became the highest-grossing film of all time with $2.798 billion, surpassing Avatar's original run.
Tim Burton's Batman (1989) was the first film to earn $100 million in its first ten days of release.
Daredevil on Netflix pushed the boundaries of superhero television with its critically acclaimed one-take hallway fight scenes.
The Mission: Impossible franchise has earned over $3.5 billion worldwide across its multiple installments.
Roger Moore played James Bond in seven films, the most of any actor in the franchise.
Extraction (2020) featured an 11-minute-long action sequence designed to look like a single unbroken shot through the streets of Dhaka.
Top Gun: Maverick (2022) became Tom Cruise's first film to gross over $1 billion at the worldwide box office.
The Batman (2022) directed by Matt Reeves presented a noir-detective version of the character, with Robert Pattinson in the lead role.
Sylvester Stallone suffered a real injury during Rocky IV when Dolph Lundgren hit him so hard in the chest that his heart swelled and he spent four days in intensive care.
Fast Five (2011) added Dwayne Johnson to the Fast & Furious franchise and shifted the series from street racing to heist-action territory.
The Tomb Raider video game franchise spawned two film series, starring Angelina Jolie (2001, 2003) and Alicia Vikander (2018).
Banshee (Cinemax) ran for four seasons and became a cult favorite for its brutal fight choreography and neo-Western storylines.
Keanu Reeves gave away a significant portion of his Matrix earnings to the special effects and costume design teams.
The Uncharted film (2022) starred Tom Holland as a young Nathan Drake and earned over $400 million worldwide despite mixed reviews.
Arnold Schwarzenegger spoke only 74 words in The Terminator, earning roughly $21,429 per word based on his salary.
Jack Reacher (Amazon Prime) cast Alan Ritchson in the title role, who at 6'2" was much closer to the book character's 6'5" frame than Tom Cruise.
The original Rambo trilogy resulted in the creation of a Saturday morning cartoon called 'Rambo: The Force of Freedom' in 1986.
Invincible (Amazon Prime) is based on the Image Comics series by Robert Kirkman and features extreme violence unusual for animated superhero shows.
Sean Connery turned down the role of Gandalf in The Lord of the Rings because he said he didn't understand the material.
Raiders of the Lost Ark was released on June 12, 1981, and earned $389 million worldwide on a $20 million budget.
The John Wick series' world-building includes the Continental Hotel, a neutral zone for assassins governed by strict rules enforced by the High Table.
Lethal Weapon 2 (1989) introduced the concept of 'diplomatic immunity' as a plot device, leading to one of the most quoted villain lines in action cinema.
Mad Max: Fury Road won six Academy Awards at the 88th ceremony, including Best Film Editing and Best Costume Design.
Pierce Brosnan's GoldenEye (1995) was the first Bond film to earn over $350 million worldwide and revived the franchise after a six-year hiatus.
The Dark Knight (2008) earned over $1 billion worldwide, becoming the first superhero film to reach that milestone.
Bruce Willis was not the first choice for Die Hard — the role was offered to Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone, Burt Reynolds, and Richard Gere.
Jackie Chan's Police Story (1985) is considered one of the greatest action films ever made, with Chan performing all of his own incredibly dangerous stunts.
The Kingsman franchise is based on the comic book series 'The Secret Service' by Mark Millar and Dave Gibbons.
Sam Raimi's Spider-Man (2002) was the first film to earn $100 million in a single opening weekend.
The Fast & Furious franchise spans multiple continents and includes submarine chases, space travel, and a family ethos that became a beloved internet meme.
Henry Cavill was director Zack Snyder's top choice for Superman in Man of Steel, after previously losing the role of Bond to Daniel Craig.
Predator's cast included two future U.S. governors: Arnold Schwarzenegger (California) and Jesse Ventura (Minnesota).
Tom Cruise scaled the Burj Khalifa, the world's tallest building at 2,717 feet, for Mission: Impossible – Ghost Protocol.
The animated series Invincible was renewed through Season 4 based on its critical acclaim and massive viewership.
The A-Team (2010) attempted to bring the classic 1980s TV show to the big screen but underperformed at the box office despite a strong cast.
Quentin Tarantino's Kill Bill was originally intended as a single film but was split into two volumes due to its nearly four-hour runtime.
Avengers: Infinity War and Endgame were filmed back-to-back over 200+ days, one of the longest continuous film shoots in history.
Jason Bourne's real name in the franchise is David Webb, a detail revealed in The Bourne Ultimatum.
The Pirates of the Caribbean franchise has grossed over $4.5 billion worldwide across five films.
Stunt coordinator Chad Stahelski, who directed all four John Wick films, was Keanu Reeves' stunt double in The Matrix.
Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice (2016) divided critics and audiences but earned $873 million worldwide.
Bruce Lee only completed four major martial arts films before his death at age 32 in 1973.
The 24 TV series produced a total of 192 episodes over eight seasons plus a limited event series called 24: Live Another Day.
Hugh Jackman played Wolverine across nine films over 17 years, from X-Men (2000) to Logan (2017).
Die Hard has been the subject of an ongoing cultural debate about whether it qualifies as a Christmas movie — Bruce Willis said it isn't.
National Treasure (2004) turned the concept of a treasure hunt involving American historical documents into a family-friendly action-adventure franchise.
Logan (2017) became the first superhero film to receive an Academy Award nomination for Best Adapted Screenplay.
The original Jumanji (1995) starring Robin Williams used groundbreaking CGI for its time but relied heavily on practical effects.
Dwayne Johnson and Jason Statham's Hobbs & Shaw (2019) was the first Fast & Furious spinoff film.
John Woo's Hard Boiled (1992) set the standard for Hong Kong action cinema with its legendary hospital shootout sequence.
Arnold Schwarzenegger and Sylvester Stallone were fierce rivals in the 1980s and '90s, each trying to outdo the other's box office records.
The Bourne Identity's car chase through Paris was filmed using a modified Mini Cooper and was largely performed without CGI.
Matt Reeves' The Batman featured nearly three hours of runtime, making it one of the longest superhero films ever produced.
Top Gun (1986) was released on May 16, 1986, and became the highest-grossing film of that year.
The stunt team for Mad Max: Fury Road trained for months in the Namibian desert, enduring extreme temperatures during filming.
Christopher Nolan refused to use a second unit director for The Dark Knight trilogy, personally directing every single shot in all three films.
The MCU's Phase One concluded with The Avengers (2012), which earned $1.5 billion worldwide and proved the shared universe concept could work.
Con Air featured a cast including Nicolas Cage, John Cusack, John Malkovich, Steve Buscemi, Ving Rhames, and Dave Chappelle.
Mission: Impossible's iconic mask-removal scenes use practical prosthetic effects combined with digital enhancement.
Keanu Reeves was offered the role of Wolverine in X-Men but turned it down.
The Expendables franchise was designed as a love letter to 1980s and '90s action films, assembling the era's biggest stars in one film.
Jackie Chan's Drunken Master (1978) popularized the 'drunken fighting' style in martial arts cinema and made him a star in Asia.
Black Panther (2018) became the first superhero film nominated for Best Picture at the Academy Awards.
James Bond's favorite drink, the vodka martini 'shaken, not stirred,' was first spoken on screen in Goldfinger (1964).
The Dark Knight's Joker was Heath Ledger's final complete performance; he passed away during post-production and won a posthumous Oscar.
Harrison Ford was working as a carpenter when George Lucas cast him as Han Solo in Star Wars, which led to his role as Indiana Jones.
Extraction 2 (2023) opened with a 21-minute single-take action sequence that is one of the longest in film history.
The James Bond theme song has become one of the most recognizable musical motifs in cinema, composed by Monty Norman and arranged by John Barry.
Rambo: Last Blood (2019) marked Stallone's fifth and reportedly final appearance as John Rambo, spanning nearly 40 years.
Avengers: Age of Ultron introduced the characters of Vision, Scarlet Witch, and Quicksilver to the MCU.
Face/Off director John Woo brought his signature 'gun ballet' and slow-motion dove shots from Hong Kong cinema to Hollywood.
Lara Croft from Tomb Raider was one of the first female action-adventure protagonists in video games, debuting in 1996.
Timothy Dalton brought a darker, more serious tone to James Bond in The Living Daylights (1987) and Licence to Kill (1989).
The Raid 2 (2014) expanded the scope of the original film from a single building to an epic crime saga while maintaining its brutal martial arts choreography.
The Dark Knight Rises concluded Nolan's Batman trilogy with a theatrical release on July 20, 2012.
Daredevil's season one hallway fight scene was performed by Charlie Cox and crew in a genuine single take with no hidden cuts.
Tom Cruise broke his ankle jumping between buildings during the filming of Mission: Impossible – Fallout, and the take was used in the final film.
The original X-Men trilogy grossed over $1.2 billion worldwide, establishing the franchise as a major Hollywood property.
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade (1989) paired Harrison Ford with Sean Connery as father and son in one of the most beloved duos in adventure cinema.
Speed (1994) launched Keanu Reeves into action stardom after the concept was pitched as 'Die Hard on a bus.'
George Lazenby appeared as James Bond in only one film, On Her Majesty's Secret Service (1969), despite being offered a seven-film contract.
The real USS Missouri, known as 'Mighty Mo,' was used for the climactic battle scene in Under Siege (1992).
Iron Man 3 (2013) earned over $1.2 billion worldwide, making it the fifth-highest-grossing film at that time.
Arnold Schwarzenegger was paid $0 for his cameo in The Expendables because he was still serving as Governor of California.
The Jack Ryan character has been portrayed by five different actors across multiple films and a TV series: Alec Baldwin, Harrison Ford, Ben Affleck, Chris Pine, and John Krasinski.
Mission: Impossible – Dead Reckoning Part One featured Tom Cruise riding a motorcycle off a cliff and parachuting, a stunt he performed six times.
The Batman franchise has been adapted into more films than any other superhero character, spanning over 15 theatrical releases.
Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon was the first martial arts film to win the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.
The Equalizer film series is based on the 1980s CBS television series of the same name, which starred Edward Woodward.
Blade (1998) starring Wesley Snipes was one of the first commercially successful Marvel films and proved R-rated comic adaptations could work.
Stallone directed Rocky IV, which was released at the height of Cold War tensions and featured a fight between an American and a Soviet boxer.
The John Wick franchise has expanded to include spinoffs like Ballerina and the TV series The Continental.
Wonder Woman (2017) became the highest-grossing film directed solely by a woman at the time, earning over $821 million worldwide.
Jason Statham starred in The Transporter trilogy, performing many of his own fight scenes and driving stunts.
The Avengers' Battle of New York sequence in the 2012 film was filmed largely on a purpose-built set in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
Cliffhanger (1993) opened with one of the most intense action sequences of the '90s, with Stallone dangling thousands of feet above a mountain range.
Bruce Lee's 'Game of Death' was unfinished at the time of his death, with only about 40 minutes of fight footage completed.
Pierce Brosnan's tenure as Bond included four films: GoldenEye, Tomorrow Never Dies, The World Is Not Enough, and Die Another Day.
Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018) won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature and revolutionized animation techniques.
The original Die Hard script was adapted from the novel 'Nothing Lasts Forever,' making John McClane originally a character named Joe Leland.
Schwarzenegger's Total Recall (1990) was one of the most expensive films ever made at the time, with a budget of approximately $65 million.
Casino Royale (2006) featured one of the most intense poker sequences in film history, shot over several weeks.
The Indiana Jones franchise's iconic boulder chase in Raiders was achieved with a fiberglass boulder rolling toward Harrison Ford downhill.
The Hobbs & Shaw spinoff earned $759 million worldwide, nearly matching several mainline Fast & Furious entries.
Deadpool (2016) became the highest-grossing R-rated film at the time with $783 million, proving R-rated superhero films could dominate the box office.
John Woo's signature white doves have appeared in nearly all of his action films, symbolizing peace amid violence.
The Mandalorian expanded the Star Wars action-adventure universe on Disney+, becoming one of the most-watched streaming series globally.
Commando (1985) features Arnold Schwarzenegger's character killing 81 people on screen, one of the highest body counts for a single character in any film.
Christopher Reeve's Superman (1978) set the standard for superhero films with its tagline 'You'll believe a man can fly.'
The Kingsman franchise used Colin Firth, typically known for dramatic and romantic roles, as an action star to surprising critical and audience acclaim.
Mission: Impossible II (2000) was directed by John Woo and is the most stylistically distinct entry in the franchise.
The Hurt Locker director Kathryn Bigelow became the first woman to win the Academy Award for Best Director with her intense action-thriller.
Keanu Reeves donated $31.5 million of his Matrix back-end profits to the film's special effects and costume teams.
The original Ghostbusters (1984) blended action, comedy, and supernatural adventure, earning $295 million worldwide.
Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014) starred Chris Pine and was Kenneth Branagh's attempt to reboot the Tom Clancy franchise.
The Boys has been praised for its satirical take on corporate superhero culture and has spawned the animated spinoff Diabolical.
Lethal Weapon's 'too old for this' catchphrase by Danny Glover became one of the most quoted lines in action movie history.
Wonder Woman 1984 (2020) was released simultaneously in theaters and on HBO Max during the COVID-19 pandemic.
The Matrix (1999) introduced 'bullet time' visual effects to mainstream cinema, revolutionizing action filmmaking.
Jackie Chan's Rush Hour franchise with Chris Tucker grossed over $840 million worldwide across three films.
Skyfall (2012) became the first James Bond film to earn over $1 billion at the worldwide box office.
Kurt Russell starred in Escape from New York (1981) and Big Trouble in Little China (1986), both directed by John Carpenter and both now cult action classics.
The Ip Man franchise spans four main films plus spinoffs and introduced Donnie Yen as a martial arts film icon to Western audiences.
Furious 7 (2015) used CGI and body doubles of Paul Walker's brothers to complete the film after Walker's tragic death during production.
Robert Pattinson's audition for The Batman involved wearing the Val Kilmer-era Batsuit since the new suit hadn't been designed yet.
The Terminator franchise has generated over $2 billion at the global box office across its six theatrical releases.
Harrison Ford has said that Indiana Jones is his favorite character to play because he gets to be both funny and heroic.
Point Break (1991) starred Keanu Reeves and Patrick Swayze and is considered one of the definitive surf-action films.
The James Bond franchise has produced 25 official Eon Productions films spanning over 60 years.
Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse (2023) expanded the multiverse concept and featured over 280 distinct Spider-characters.
Denzel Washington prepared for The Equalizer by training in Krav Maga and studying the mannerisms of retired special operations soldiers.
The Expendables 2 (2012) added Chuck Norris to its already stacked cast, including a self-referential Chuck Norris joke in the film.
Batman Returns (1992) was considered too dark and violent for its merchandise tie-ins, leading Warner Bros. to hire Joel Schumacher for a lighter approach in Batman Forever.
The Bourne Supremacy (2004) popularized the 'shaky cam' shooting style that became ubiquitous in action films throughout the 2000s.
Nicolas Cage turned down the role of Aragorn in The Lord of the Rings to focus on his family.
Aquaman (2018) became the first DC Extended Universe film to cross $1 billion at the worldwide box office.
Schwarzenegger's famous 'Get to the chopper!' line from Predator has been sampled, remixed, and parodied countless times in popular culture.
The Transporter (2002) established Jason Statham as an action star and launched a franchise that included three films and a TV series.
The Dark Knight's opening bank heist sequence was partially inspired by the opening of Michael Mann's Heat (1995).
Bruce Willis starred in a total of five Die Hard films between 1988 and 2013.
Mission: Impossible III (2006) was J.J. Abrams' feature film directorial debut, bringing his TV-style intensity to the big screen.
Roger Moore was 57 years old when he made his final Bond film, A View to a Kill (1985), making him the oldest actor to play 007.
The MCU's Black Widow (2021) was eventually released both in theaters and on Disney+ with Premier Access after multiple COVID-related delays.
Jackie Chan won an honorary Oscar at the 2016 Governors Awards after appearing in over 200 films throughout his career.
Stallone wrote, directed, and starred in Rocky Balboa (2006), reviving the franchise 16 years after Rocky V.
Gladiator (2000) won five Academy Awards including Best Picture and Best Actor for Russell Crowe.
The Mandalorian used a revolutionary virtual production technique called 'The Volume,' using massive LED screens instead of green screens.
No Time to Die (2021) was Daniel Craig's final Bond film and earned $774 million worldwide despite pandemic conditions.
The original Mortal Kombat (1995) film was one of the first successful video game-to-film adaptations.
Peaky Blinders ran for six seasons over nine years, from 2013 to 2022, on BBC and Netflix.
Tom Hardy's performance as Bane in The Dark Knight Rises required a voice modulator mask, which was adjusted in post-production after early audience complaints.
John Wick: Chapter 4 (2023) earned over $440 million worldwide, the highest in the franchise.
The practical car flips in Casino Royale's DBS sequence earned a Guinness World Record for the most cannon rolls in a car.
Kill Bill's Bride character uses a Hattori Hanzo sword, named after a famous historical Japanese samurai and ninja.
Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) proved that lesser-known Marvel characters could carry a blockbuster film, earning $773 million worldwide.
Steven Spielberg and George Lucas collaborated on all four original Indiana Jones films, with Spielberg directing and Lucas producing.
The Fast & Furious franchise's 'family' theme became so iconic it spawned major internet memes about Vin Diesel's character Dom Toretto.
Atomic Blonde (2017) starred Charlize Theron performing many of her own stunts, with a brutal stairwell fight scene that took weeks to film.
The Expendables franchise grossed over $800 million across four films by assembling legends of the action genre.
Schwarzenegger's catchphrase 'Hasta la vista, baby' from Terminator 2 was dubbed differently in the Spanish version to avoid redundancy — he says 'Sayonara, baby' instead.
Doctor Strange (2016) brought psychedelic visuals and mind-bending action to the MCU, inspired by the works of Steve Ditko.
The Transporter's rules — never change the deal, no names, never open the package — became a defining character trait for Jason Statham's Frank Martin.
Daniel Craig performed many of his own stunts in the Bond films, including the parkour-inspired chase sequence in Casino Royale.
The original Fast and the Furious (2001) was loosely based on a Vibe magazine article about illegal street racing in New York.
Stallone and Schwarzenegger both invested in the Planet Hollywood restaurant chain in the 1990s alongside Bruce Willis.
Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014) incorporated political thriller elements inspired by 1970s films like Three Days of the Condor.
Sicario (2015) featured a tense border crossing sequence shot in a single continuous take by cinematographer Roger Deakins.
The John Wick franchise's 'pencil kill' was inspired by the Joker's magic trick in The Dark Knight, according to the filmmakers.
Mission: Impossible (1996) was released on May 22, 1996, and earned $457 million worldwide, launching one of Hollywood's most durable franchises.
The Matrix Reloaded's highway chase scene took three months to film on a specially constructed 1.5-mile stretch of freeway.
Blade Runner 2049 (2017) was a sequel to Ridley Scott's 1982 original and starred Ryan Gosling and Harrison Ford.
Banshee's fight scenes were choreographed by stunt coordinators from the John Wick franchise, giving the show a distinctive brutal style.
Thor: Ragnarok (2017) was directed by Taika Waititi, who reinvented the Thor franchise with a comedic, colorful tone.
The Pirates of the Caribbean franchise features Johnny Depp's Captain Jack Sparrow, a character inspired by Keith Richards of the Rolling Stones.
James Cameron's Aliens (1986) is considered one of the greatest action sequels ever made, shifting the franchise from horror to military sci-fi action.
The Meg (2018) starring Jason Statham earned $530 million worldwide, proving the appeal of straightforward creature-action blockbusters.
Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings (2021) was the MCU's first film with an Asian lead, starring Simu Liu.
Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny (2023) marked Harrison Ford's fifth and final appearance as the iconic archaeologist, at age 80.
Donnie Yen was added to the John Wick: Chapter 4 cast as Caine, a blind assassin, bringing his martial arts expertise to the franchise.
The running time of all MCU Infinity Saga films combined exceeds 48 hours of content.
Stallone filmed the famous Rocky training montage running up the steps of the Philadelphia Museum of Art, which are now called the 'Rocky Steps.'
The Crow (1994) became legendary after star Brandon Lee was accidentally killed on set by a prop gun loaded with a dummy round containing a real bullet.
Under Siege (1992) was essentially 'Die Hard on a ship' and was one of Steven Seagal's biggest box office hits.
The Suicide Squad (2021) was written and directed by James Gunn, who brought his Guardians of the Galaxy humor to DC.
In Terminator 2, the T-1000 was played by Robert Patrick, who trained relentlessly to run at sprinting speed without appearing winded.
GoldenEye 007 for the Nintendo 64 (1997) became one of the best-selling and most influential first-person shooters ever, directly tied to the Bond film's popularity.
The Bourne Legacy (2012) starred Jeremy Renner and expanded the Bourne universe without Matt Damon, though the film received mixed reviews.
Wrath of Man (2021) reunited Jason Statham with director Guy Ritchie, marking their first collaboration since 2005's Revolver.
Chris Evans was initially reluctant to take the role of Captain America because he feared being typecast, but eventually accepted after multiple offers.
Lethal Weapon (1987) was released on March 6, 1987, and earned $120 million domestically.
The Batman's Batmobile in Matt Reeves' film was designed as a modified muscle car rather than a military vehicle, reflecting the film's grounded tone.
Tom Cruise reportedly runs at full speed in every Mission: Impossible film, and his running form has become iconic among fans and filmmakers.
Deadpool & Wolverine (2024) brought Hugh Jackman back as Wolverine and was the first Deadpool film produced under Marvel Studios.
Stunt coordinator Vic Armstrong doubled for Harrison Ford in the Indiana Jones and James Bond franchises and holds a Guinness record as the world's most prolific stuntman.
The car chase in Bullitt (1968) starring Steve McQueen is widely considered the first great car chase in cinema and influenced every action film that followed.
Zack Snyder's Justice League (the 'Snyder Cut') was released on HBO Max in 2021 after years of fan campaigning, running over four hours.
Tomb Raider (2018) starring Alicia Vikander rebooted the franchise with a more grounded, survival-oriented tone inspired by the 2013 video game.
The John Wick franchise uses a system of gold coins as currency in its underground assassin world, and the coins have become popular collector's items.
Edge of Tomorrow (2014) starred Tom Cruise and Emily Blunt in a sci-fi action film where Cruise's character relives the same battle over and over.
Jackie Chan's Project A (1983) featured a fall from a clock tower inspired by Harold Lloyd, performed without any safety equipment.
The Avengers (2012) was the third-highest-grossing film of all time upon release and was the culmination of Marvel's Phase One.
Ben Affleck's Batman in Batman v Superman was the first live-action portrayal to show Batman using armored combat suits.
Olympus Has Fallen (2013) and White House Down (2013) were both 'Die Hard in the White House' films released within months of each other.
Tony Jaa's Ong-Bak (2003) showcased Muay Thai fighting on film with no wires or CGI, earning Jaa comparisons to a young Bruce Lee.
The original Planet of the Apes (1968) blended science fiction with action-adventure and featured one of cinema's greatest twist endings.
Captain America: Civil War (2016) functioned as essentially an Avengers film, pitting Iron Man against Captain America and introducing Spider-Man and Black Panther to the MCU.
Jason Statham's Crank (2006) was essentially a live-action video game, with Statham's character needing constant adrenaline to stay alive.
The Fast & Furious franchise has grossed over $7 billion worldwide across its mainline entries and spinoffs.
Liam Neeson was 56 years old when Taken was released, making him one of the oldest actors to launch a major action franchise.
Schwarzenegger was paid just $75,000 for his role in The Terminator (1984), which went on to earn $78 million worldwide.
The Punisher first appeared in Marvel Comics as a Spider-Man villain before becoming an antihero starring in his own action-driven comics, films, and TV series.
Keanu Reeves trained with Taran Tactical for the John Wick films, and real competition shooters have praised his firearms handling as genuinely expert-level.
Ryan Reynolds spent over a decade trying to get Deadpool made, leaking test footage online in 2014 that generated such positive buzz the studio greenlit the film.
Michelle Yeoh performed most of her own stunts in Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon despite tearing her ACL during production.
The Mummy (1999) blended Indiana Jones-style adventure with horror elements and grossed $415 million worldwide.
The One (2001) starring Jet Li explored the multiverse concept in martial arts cinema long before it became a mainstream trend.
Chad Stahelski's background as a martial artist and stunt performer directly shaped John Wick's distinctive fighting style, emphasizing practical combat over wirework.
Tom Cruise has performed increasingly dangerous stunts with each Mission: Impossible installment, with insurance companies reportedly struggling to cover him.
The Dark Knight's pencil trick scene was achieved practically — the Joker slams a goon's head onto a table, and the pencil retracts through a hidden hole.
Gladiator 2 (2024) brought Ridley Scott back to direct a sequel set decades after the events of the original film.
John McTiernan directed both Die Hard (1988) and Predator (1987), cementing himself as one of the defining action directors of the era.
Martial arts legend Jet Li has appeared in both Hollywood and Hong Kong action films, bridging the gap between Eastern and Western action cinema.
The longest car chase in Fast & Furious history occurs in Fast Five, lasting over 15 minutes as Dom and Brian drag a bank vault through Rio's streets.
Jack Reacher (2012) starred Tom Cruise despite significant fan controversy over the casting, as the book character is described as 6'5" and 250 pounds.
Aliens featured Sigourney Weaver as Ellen Ripley, one of the most iconic female action heroes in cinema history.
The Raid (2011) takes place almost entirely within a single apartment building in Jakarta, with the hero fighting floor by floor to reach a crime lord.
Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior (1981) established the post-apocalyptic action genre template that countless films and video games have imitated.
Aaron Taylor-Johnson was cast as the new James Bond, continuing the franchise's tradition of reinvention with each new actor.
Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest (2006) earned $1.066 billion worldwide and was the fastest film to reach $1 billion at that time.
Die Hard 2 (1990) was set at Washington Dulles International Airport, though the exterior shots were actually filmed at a different airport.
The Black Panther sequel Wakanda Forever (2022) honored the legacy of Chadwick Boseman, who passed away from colon cancer in 2020.
Statham, Stallone, and Schwarzenegger have all appeared together in The Expendables franchise, representing three eras of action cinema.
The Matrix used over 100 cameras arranged in a circle for its bullet-time sequences, capturing the actors from every angle simultaneously.
John Woo directed Mission: Impossible II and added his signature motorcycle chase and slo-mo dual-wielding gun sequences to the franchise.
The Indiana Jones trilogy was completed in 1989 with The Last Crusade before a fourth film, Kingdom of the Crystal Skull, arrived 19 years later in 2008.
Total Recall (1990) contains a famous scene where Schwarzenegger disguises himself as a woman, using a practical animatronic head.
Shang-Chi's bus fight sequence was inspired by classic Jackie Chan action scenes and is considered one of the MCU's best action set pieces.
The Predator franchise has crossed over with the Alien franchise in two Alien vs. Predator films (2004, 2007), uniting two iconic sci-fi action properties.
The first Avengers film assembled Iron Man, Captain America, Thor, Hulk, Black Widow, and Hawkeye — characters introduced across five preceding films.
Live and Let Die (1973) was Roger Moore's first Bond film and featured one of the franchise's most famous stunts: a speedboat jump over a road.
The stunts in Mission: Impossible – Ghost Protocol were largely performed at the real Burj Khalifa with Tom Cruise climbing the exterior.
Prey (2022) set a Predator film in the Comanche Nation territory of 1719, earning the best reviews the franchise had received since the original.
The Kill Bill franchise was heavily influenced by Shaw Brothers martial arts films, Italian spaghetti westerns, and Japanese samurai cinema.
Die Hard with a Vengeance (1995) paired Bruce Willis with Samuel L. Jackson and moved the action across New York City in a citywide cat-and-mouse game.
Gal Gadot served two years in the Israel Defense Forces before her acting career, which prepared her physically for the role of Wonder Woman.
Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) was groundbreaking for its use of CGI to create the liquid metal T-1000.
Dwayne Johnson's Jumanji: The Next Level (2019) earned $800 million worldwide, continuing the rebooted franchise's box office success.
The MCU introduced 130+ major characters across its Infinity Saga, each woven into a single interconnected narrative.
Hard Target (1993) was John Woo's first Hollywood film, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme.
The Revenant (2015) featured Tom Hardy and Leonardo DiCaprio in a grueling survival-adventure film shot entirely in natural light.
Peaky Blinders spawned a spin-off film announced to continue the story beyond the TV series finale.
The Magnificent Seven (2016) was a remake of the classic 1960 Western, which was itself a remake of Akira Kurosawa's Seven Samurai.
Stallone was so dedicated to Rocky that he refused a $360,000 offer for the script unless he could star in it, despite being nearly broke at the time.
Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017) was the first Spider-Man film produced in collaboration between Sony and Marvel Studios.
The A-Team TV series ran from 1983 to 1987 and starred Mr. T as B.A. Baracus, creating one of television's most iconic action characters.
The Fast & Furious franchise launched the careers of several actors, including Jordana Brewster, Michelle Rodriguez, and Ludacris.
John Wick's signature pencil-killing technique became so iconic that it spawned countless memes and references in other media.
Schwarzenegger returned to the franchise with Terminator 3: Rise of the Machines in 2003 after an 11-year gap, earning $30 million.
The Matrix Resurrections (2021) was directed by Lana Wachowski without her sister Lilly for the first time.
Mad Max: Furiosa (2024) served as a prequel exploring the origins of Charlize Theron's character, with Anya Taylor-Joy in the younger role.
Daniel Craig's tenure as Bond lasted 15 years and five films, the longest span of any actor in the role.
The Expendables 4 (2023) introduced a new generation of action stars alongside franchise veterans like Stallone and Statham.
Chadwick Boseman secretly battled colon cancer while filming Black Panther, several Avengers films, and other projects over a four-year period.
The '80s action movie boom was fueled by the home video market, with VHS sales and rentals often exceeding theatrical box office returns.
Heat (1995) featured the first on-screen pairing of Robert De Niro and Al Pacino in the same scene, during a diner conversation shot from multiple angles over several days.
The Lord of the Rings trilogy blended epic adventure with action sequences, winning 17 Academy Awards across the three films.
Ip Man 4: The Finale (2019) concluded the franchise with Donnie Yen's character traveling to the United States.
The MCU's Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 (2023) was James Gunn's final Marvel film before he became co-head of DC Studios.
Daredevil: Born Again on Disney+ brought Charlie Cox back in the role and was one of the most anticipated Marvel TV shows.
The original Superman film (1978) starring Christopher Reeve cost $55 million to produce, making it one of the most expensive films of its era.
Bruce Willis's performance in Die Hard created the template for the 'everyman action hero,' replacing the muscle-bound leads of '80s action cinema.
True Lies (1994) was directed by James Cameron and starred Schwarzenegger as a spy hiding his profession from his wife, played by Jamie Lee Curtis.
The Bond franchise has used over 100 different cars across its films, with the Aston Martin DB5 being the most iconic.
Dwayne Johnson started his acting career with The Mummy Returns (2001) and its spinoff The Scorpion King (2002), launching from WWE star to Hollywood leading man.
Dark Knight trilogy cinematographer Wally Pfister won an Oscar for Inception and helped define the visual language of modern superhero cinema.
The Bourne franchise's shaky-cam fight choreography was so influential that the James Bond franchise adopted similar techniques starting with Casino Royale.
Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle was released on December 20, 2017, directly competing with Star Wars: The Last Jedi at the box office.
The original Terminator (1984) was made on a modest $6.4 million budget and earned $78 million worldwide.
The TV series Reacher on Amazon Prime became one of the streaming platform's most-watched shows, earning immediate renewal.
Steven Seagal's Under Siege was the actor's biggest commercial hit, earning $156 million worldwide on a $35 million budget.
The climactic battle in Avengers: Endgame featured nearly every hero introduced in the MCU, requiring an unprecedented coordination of visual effects teams.
Schwarzenegger's 'consider that a divorce' line in Total Recall was improvised on set and became one of his most quoted one-liners.
Captain Marvel (2019) was the MCU's first female-led superhero film, earning over $1.1 billion worldwide.
The original Resident Evil film (2002) launched a six-film franchise starring Milla Jovovich that grossed over $1.2 billion worldwide.
Dolph Lundgren holds a master's degree in chemical engineering from the University of Sydney and was awarded a Fulbright scholarship to MIT before pursuing acting.
The Fast & Furious: Tokyo Drift (2006) introduced the drifting subculture to mainstream audiences and has become a cult favorite in the franchise.
Top Gun: Maverick's cast underwent three months of intensive flight training including water survival, aerobatics, and high-G force exposure.
The Bond film Spectre (2015) featured a single-take opening sequence set during Mexico City's Day of the Dead celebration that took months to plan.
Kick-Ass (2010) featured an 11-year-old Chloë Grace Moretz as Hit-Girl, generating controversy for the character's extreme violence and language.
Pan's Labyrinth (2006) blended dark fantasy adventure with the horrors of the Spanish Civil War, earning three Academy Awards.
The original Ben-Hur (1959) featured a chariot race that took five weeks to film and used 15,000 extras on the largest film set ever built at the time.
Ryan Gosling performed many of his own stunts in The Fall Guy (2024), a film paying homage to the stunt profession.
Kevin Hart and Dwayne Johnson's on-screen chemistry led to multiple action-comedy collaborations including Central Intelligence and the Jumanji sequels.
The Bond film GoldenEye (1995) also spawned one of the most beloved video games of all time on the Nintendo 64.
Kurt Russell and Sylvester Stallone starred together in Tango & Cash (1989), a buddy cop film that was plagued by production problems but became a cult hit.
The first Iron Man film was largely improvised, with Robert Downey Jr. and Jeff Bridges ad-libbing much of the dialogue since the script was still being written during filming.
Stallone broke his neck while performing a wrestling stunt with 'Stone Cold' Steve Austin in The Expendables.
Charlie's Angels (2000) brought the 1970s TV show to the big screen with Cameron Diaz, Drew Barrymore, and Lucy Liu.
The original Jurassic Park (1993) blended adventure with groundbreaking CGI dinosaurs, earning $1.03 billion worldwide.
Tony Stark's snap in Avengers: Endgame was not in the original script — Robert Downey Jr. improvised the 'I am Iron Man' callback during reshoots.
The 007 franchise's Q Branch gadgets have included everything from exploding pens to invisible cars, capturing audiences' imaginations for decades.
Jason Bourne's amnesia-driven quest for identity across the trilogy set a new template for spy thrillers that prioritized realism over gadgetry.
Schwarzenegger famously said he was drawn to action roles because the genre allowed him to overcome his Austrian accent with short, punchy dialogue.
The Avengers' shawarma post-credits scene was filmed just one day after the film's world premiere, with Chris Evans hiding a beard behind his hand.
Die Hard (1988) was released on July 15, 1988, and earned $140 million worldwide on a $28 million budget.
Jean-Claude Van Damme's splits between two moving trucks in a Volvo commercial became one of the most viewed ads in internet history, inspired by his action film stunts.
The Wolverine (2013) was set primarily in Japan and drew heavily from Chris Claremont's celebrated 1982 comic book miniseries.
Stallone turned down the role of John McClane in Die Hard, which eventually went to Bruce Willis and launched one of the biggest action franchises ever.
The helicopter stunt in Cliffhanger was performed at an altitude of 4,000 feet in the Italian Dolomites and cost over $1 million to film.
Black Adam (2022) starred Dwayne Johnson, who had campaigned for the role for over 15 years before it was finally produced.
The final fight in Rocky III between Rocky and Clubber Lang (Mr. T) was shot in just two days.
Jason Momoa transformed Aquaman from a frequently mocked superhero into a box office powerhouse.
The Die Hard franchise spawned multiple imitators described by their pitches as 'Die Hard on a [location],' including Speed, Under Siege, and Cliffhanger.
Salt (2010) was originally written for Tom Cruise as a male lead but was rewritten for Angelina Jolie, one of the few female-led spy action films of its era.
Guy Ritchie directed two Sherlock Holmes action-adventure films with Robert Downey Jr. that earned over $1 billion combined.
Keanu Reeves personally designed several of the tactical gear pieces used in John Wick: Chapter 3 – Parabellum.
The opening parkour chase in Casino Royale was inspired by real-life freerunner Sébastien Foucan, who actually appears in the sequence.
The Night Manager (2016) starred Tom Hiddleston in a John le Carré spy thriller so well received that Hiddleston was briefly considered for James Bond.
Bloodsport (1988) was Jean-Claude Van Damme's breakout film, loosely based on the alleged true story of Frank Dux and the underground Kumite tournament.
Iron Man 2 introduced Scarlett Johansson as Black Widow, a role she played across ten Marvel films over 11 years.
Fury (2014) starred Brad Pitt in a WWII tank action film where the cast lived inside a real WWII tank for a week to prepare for their roles.
300 (2006) used extensive green screen and digital backgrounds to achieve its hyper-stylized visual look inspired by Frank Miller's graphic novel.
Schwarzenegger's Kindergarten Cop (1990) proved he could succeed in lighter action-comedy roles while maintaining his action star status.
Dredd (2012) starring Karl Urban is widely considered a cult classic and a faithful adaptation of the Judge Dredd comics, despite underperforming at the box office.
The Rundown (2003) featured a brief cameo by Arnold Schwarzenegger passing the torch to Dwayne Johnson, symbolizing a generational shift in action cinema.
John Wick: Chapter 2 expanded the assassin mythology with the introduction of the Marker, a blood oath that cannot be refused.
The Bridge on the River Kwai (1957) won seven Academy Awards and remains one of the greatest adventure war films ever made.
Scarlett Johansson's Lucy (2014) earned $463 million worldwide, proving she could carry an action film as a solo lead.
Upgrade (2018) was made on just a $5 million budget and earned praise for its inventive action sequences and dark sci-fi premise.
The Witcher series on Netflix combined fantasy adventure with intense sword choreography, with Henry Cavill performing many of his own fight scenes.
Mission: Impossible – Rogue Nation was released on July 31, 2015, and earned $682 million worldwide.
Ang Lee directed Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon using wirework choreographed by legendary action director Yuen Woo-ping, who also choreographed The Matrix.
Stallone has written or co-written every Rambo and Rocky film he has appeared in, maintaining creative control throughout both franchises.
